title: Performance, scalability, availability
intro: The non-functional big four — and why you measure them at the tail, not the average.

steps.title: Response time has two parts
steps.body:
**Response time = processing time + waiting time.**

A request computed in 5 ms can still take 800 ms because it sat in a queue. Optimising the algorithm does nothing if **waiting** dominates — you fix that with concurrency, queuing and capacity, not faster code.

**Throughput** (requests/sec) is a related but distinct goal from latency.

steps.title: Percentiles beat averages
steps.body:
An average hides the pain. If 99% of requests are fast and 1% take 10 s, the mean looks fine — but that 1% is often your most engaged users.

**p99 = 200 ms** means '99% of requests finish within 200 ms' — a promise about the tail. SLA and alert on **p99**, never the average.

points.label: avg
points.note: Mean. Hides outliers — do not SLA on this.

points.label: p50
points.note: Median. Says nothing about the worst experiences.

points.label: p95
points.note: 95% finish within this. The tail begins to show.

points.label: p99
points.note: 99% finish within this. THIS is what you promise — your heaviest users' experience.

steps.title: Scale up vs scale out
steps.body:
- **Vertical (up)** — a bigger machine. Simple, but bounded by the biggest box, and a single point of failure.
- **Horizontal (out)** — more machines behind a load balancer. Effectively unbounded and doubles as redundancy, but forces **statelessness, data distribution, coordination**.

> [!tip] Large-scale systems favour **horizontal** scaling — the only path past one machine's ceiling. The price is complexity.

steps.title: Availability = tolerating failure
steps.body:
**Availability = MTBF / (MTBF + MTTR)** — raise it by failing less *or* recovering faster. Each extra 'nine' cuts allowed downtime ~10× (99.9% ≈ 8.76 h/yr; 99.99% ≈ 53 min/yr).

You hit the target with **redundancy** — no single point of failure: spatial (copies across zones), time (retry idempotent ops), active-active (all serve) or active-passive (standby fails over).

recap:
**Measure quality attributes at the tail; buy availability with redundancy.**

- Response time = **processing + waiting**; often the wait dominates.
- SLA on **p99**, not the average; stay left of the latency **knee**.
- Prefer **horizontal** scaling; accept the statelessness cost.
- Availability = MTBF/(MTBF+MTTR); each nine ≈ 10× less downtime, met by **redundancy**.
